title: Cum îl înfundă Voineag pe Predoiu. L-a ales Predoiu personal peste capul comisiei, așa cum ne informa Ioana Dogioiu?
summary: The article examines allegations that Justice Minister Predoiu bypassed the official selection commission to appoint Marius Voineag as head of DNA (National Anticorruption Directorate), despite the commission recommending former DNA chief Crin Bologa. Author Cristian Ghinea argues this represents a politically motivated capture of justice institutions, citing journalist Ioana Dogioiu's earlier reporting that Voineag was chosen for helping Nicolae Ciucă escape a plagiarism verdict. Ghinea announces he will file a parliamentary interpellation and a freedom of information request to obtain the commission's actual decision.
talking_points: Voineag claims he was selected by the commission, but former DNA chief Bologa and journalist Dogioiu both contradict this, saying Predoiu overrode the commission's recommendation
Voineag allegedly helped Ciucă escape a plagiarism verdict by assigning the case to a prosecutor near retirement for an already-prescribed offense
The political accountability of the Justice Minister for choosing chief prosecutors is a fundamental democratic principle that was violated
Ghinea announces parliamentary interpellation and FOIA request to reveal the commission's actual decision
The article connects Voineag's appointment to a broader pattern of political capture of Romania's justice system after the EU's MCV monitoring was lifted
author: Cristian Ghinea
